programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies is the 'Taskmaster' in this game show that challenges the wit and wisdom of five comedians, who are all putting their reputations on the line. Alex Horne assists Davies in setting up unique and ridiculous tasks for the competitors.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: In London, will Jody and Shane's million-pound budget be enough for a slice of the Big Smoke? And Kirstie gives foodies Roopal and Hershil all the ingredients for an ideal home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Country Lives
programme.desc: James and Jodie are pushing to get one room habitable in their dilapidated old farmhouse, while Dave learns how to keep show sheep.

programme.title: Escape to Florida
programme.desc: Lea swaps Liverpool life for the Horse and Jockey pub in South Pasadena, and 74-year-old Londoner Lynne has moved to the largest retirement community in the world - The Villages.

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Alicante, the Rodriguezes are dealt an unexpected blow by a fast-moving property market. In Murcia, the Browns swing into action for golf-fan Brian and Nikki.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Steve and Linda have been visiting Spain for over 10 years and feel that now's the perfect time to buy their very own holiday home on the Orihuela Costa.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Penny and Russell want a holiday home near Spain's seaside town of Mojácar, with a communal pool and just a short drive to the beach. Can Ben Hillman deliver their dream for £125k?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Paul dreams of owning a holiday home on Spain's Costa Blanca, to escape the winter months and spend quality time with family. Ben Hillman shows him what £80k can buy, but are any the 'one'?

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: A bathroom discovery gives Louisa and Roger that sinking feeling. Debbie and Dave's craft room is derailed. And will hauling a bath up three floors push Donna and Paul's limits?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Frances dreams of buying a holiday home that she can enjoy with her friends and family in Spain's beautiful coastal town of Nerja. Danni Menzies helps her find the perfect place for her budget of £200k.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The competition comes from Swansea, and the first host is DJ Kevin, who is hoping to win his guests over with an evening themed around his favourite holiday destination, Benidorm.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: This week's competition is in and around Swansea, where second host, cafe bar owner Tino, is hoping to wow her guests with an African banquet inspired by her homeland of Zimbabwe.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the third night in and around Swansea, and beautician Natalie's hoping her night goes with a Wham! as she's pulling out all the stops with a George Michael themed evening.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the fourth day of the competition in and around Swansea, and sales manager and bodybuilder Rob is hoping his `simple but tasty' Thai themed menu will win him the £1000 prize.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In and around Swansea, final host, GP and keen tennis player Janine is hoping to serve up an ace with a tennis themed evening, and the guests turn up dressed up for centre court.

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: A family of three dogs are split up to increase their chances of finding a home. Darcy the cockapoo calls out for a hero and may just find one in 11-year-old Luke.

programme.title: George Clarke's Beautiful Builds
programme.desc: In Bromley, George designs a stunning extension to include a gorgeous kitchen featuring curved arches for seasoned renovators Mitch and Elliot. An inspirational garden design is also part of their ambitious dream, but a flood derails the build.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A little boy has a stone up his nose from playing football. A 92-year-old has had a fall by her car. And World War II hero Norman is 95 and has a dangerously low heart rate.

programme.title: Emergency
programme.desc: Emergency returns as a tense four-part serial across the week. As a pedestrian is crushed by a bus and a holiday paraglide goes wrong, can the frontline trauma units save lives?

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the week's biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: What do Mo Gilligan, Babatunde Aléshé, Jon Richardson, Lucy Beaumont, Fearne Cotton, Gok Wan, Miquita and Andi Oliver, Johnny Ball and other famous faces think of our top telly?

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Can nervous Jack find common ground with Rachella? Scott plucks up the courage to shed his drag alter-ego, Casta Hex. And can DJ Priscilla and accountant Raj reconcile their musical differences?

programme.title: Love Cheats
programme.desc: A female love rat scams Tinder dates out of more than just money with a false pregnancy and a fake terminal cancer. Will she finally face justice in an Australian court?

programme.desc: Greg Davies is the 'Taskmaster' in this game show that challenges the wit and wisdom of five comedians, who are all putting their reputations on the line. Alex Horne assists Davies in setting up unique and ridiculous tasks for the competitors.

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: The fresh batch of teams face the now-iconic secret challenge and this time, it's the classic Paris Brest, with a twist. For their next challenge, the teams create childhood-toy-inspired showpieces concealing crumble and custard desserts.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A reverse ramming sends Chris reaching for german shepherd Ronny, Lee is confronted by a particularly mouthy motorist, Liam arrests a man who denies carrying out a shocking attack, tasers are drawn after a van driver causes carnage, and a wanted man holes up in a loft and refuses to come quietly.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Interceptor Spike takes the lead against a runaway car thief in one of the most challenging pursuits ever. Liam investigates a gang of suspected drug dealers. A supposedly straightforward stop snowballs into a showdown with an aggressive youngster.

programme.title: The Way Ahead
programme.desc: A lieutenant moulds a group of British civilians into a crack fighting unit to meet Rommel's forces in North Africa.

programme.title: The Devil's Brigade
programme.desc: A fighting unit in WWII made up of outcast Americans and disciplined Canadians is sent to Italy to attempt a dangerous mission that has been, up until now, an impossible mission to complete.

programme.title: The Devil's Brigade
programme.desc: A fighting unit in WWII made up of outcast Americans and disciplined Canadians is sent to Italy to attempt a dangerous mission that has been, up until now, an impossible mission to complete.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: At Crouch HQ, there is excitement in the air as the team help to make trucking superfan Chris's wedding day extra special. Also, late into the nightshift, Snod is called to recover two crashed cars from the side of the M69.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Glen rushes to help a woman trapped inside a burning car, Hayley is on patrol during `Black Eye Friday', and Ross and John are called in to help a victim who has been bound and gagged. Meanwhile, Duggie and Gareth deal with a bullock breakout, Steve gets a bad boy racer to phone his mum after misbehaving, and a motorway pursuit is bought to an abrupt end with a perfectly timed textbook shunt.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A drunken driver is in the fast lane of the M6, and dog handler Glenn is given a crash course in marine rescue when he answers an emergency distress call.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: Officers race to catch a reckless joyrider who refuses to stop. As the cops close in, the suspects run for it after crashing the car and it bursts into flames.

programme.title: Police: Night Shift 999
programme.desc: Gloucestershire police deal with the results of thousands of racegoers attending the annual festival. Drinking all day leads to trouble, and a woman is found asleep in a stranger's car.

programme.title: Cops
programme.desc: A man is caught illegally selling mixed drinks to spring breakers. A woman caught two days in a row drinking underage claims she forgot to wash out her cup.

programme.title: Cops
programme.desc: A swimmer in distress is rescued during spring break. Airbnb guests are evicted in Key West. A street performer tickles the ivories and blocks pedestrians. Bikini-clad nurses are caught with booze.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: Mulder and Scully must protect a child from a serial killer with a penchant for murdering those claiming to suffer from religious wounds.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Worlds Toughest Prisons
programme.desc: The high-security facility on the outskirts of Bucharest houses some of the most dangerous criminals, including murderers, rapists and human traffickers. Escape is near impossible.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Livestock trucker Troy Coombe comes up against severe weather and difficult passengers as he transports two ostriches to their new home.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: A tractor unit and a trailer topples over into a muddy ditch; a lorry becomes trapped with its wheels buried in mud; a 44 tonne unit loses its grip on an icy road.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: A recovery officer struggles after a truck ends up in a ditch where difficult conditions, the pressures of grow-ing traffic and an uncooperative truck driver complicate matters.

programme.title: Winter Road Rescue
programme.desc: Shot over three months, this documentary series follows the snowplough drivers and patrols who work around the clock to make sure the UK does not grind to a halt when winter weather hits. In the Highlands, snowplough teams battle blizzards, ice, flooding and landslides to keep the roads open, whilst across the UK patrols fight snow, gales and icy conditions to reach motorists stranded in the cold.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: It is a few day before Christmas, but Jason is short of festive cheer when a yuletide party becomes a full-blown riot. Adam and Nick uncover a drunk mother driving her daughter to the shops for some present-buying and Kev is seconds from disaster when he fearlessly prevents a car from becoming a fireball.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Scott and Adam discover a huge stash in the boot of a car, and a runaway dealer just misses mowing down Gary. The crime-fighting spree continues when Ady collars a car thief and the interceptors are called to a pub fight on New Year's Eve. Throw in five cars pursuing a runaway on a busy motorway and you are in for quite a ride.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Scott and Dave track a pair of mobile phone thieves with the help of GPS technology, Nick pushes his motor to the limit as he pursues a criminal gang and Jason has to deal with his own personal traffic nightmare. Elsewhere, Vicky spots a van overflowing with suspicious-looking scrap and a BMW ploughs into a house over the Christmas period.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: PCs Ian and Matt pull over a Chevvy. A search of the vehicle reveals drugs paraphernalia. In Warrington, cops the hunt for an uninsured driver who may be drunk.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: Northumbria Police deal with an angry van driver who has no MOT. And a PC faces a suspected drug driver whose vocal boyfriend won't come quietly.

programme.desc: Mulder seeks the help of a woman whom, he believes, has a special connection with a young girl who has recently been kidnapped by the same man who held her captive many years earlier.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: The agents uncover more information in the government's attempt to hide the truth about extraterrestrial life when Mulder discovers a video of an alleged alien autopsy.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: A pursuit of a reckless getaway driver in a stolen car ends with a full-scale air-to-ground search for a suspect and a battle to save his life. In Ripley, a report of a crashed car leads to the arrest of a very drunk man who, despite being found with the key to the crashed car in his pocket and being caught on CCTV moments after the crash, denies any wrongdoing.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: The traffic cops respond to a report of a car linked to a drunken assault with a baseball bat. On quarter-finals night, a simple case of a drunk and uninsured driver proves much more difficult to deal with.

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: David introduces Cash to the station's newest recruit: Probationary Constable Richard Brezniac.

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Following the residents of the small beach town of Summer Bay, as they deal with relationships, family, love, loss, and growing up.

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Observational documentary series going into the private world of local GP surgeries. An exclusive look into the every day lives of the doctors who work there, with GPs being asked more and more to cover the areas the hospitals can't.

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Sister Vicky faces a packed resus as Diane suffers a life-threatening allergic reaction, On the other hand, Allen struggles to breathe, and a four-year-old has a serious arm injury.

programme.title: 999 Critical Condition
programme.desc: A cardiac arrest patient is found to also have a perforated bowel, a gas explosion could cost a man his leg, and an investigation gets underway into what is causing a woman's blood pressure to plummet.

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: A patient in police custody is brought into hospital for treatment to a split eyebrow and some facial wounds. On arrival, the patient is aggressive towards the police and unreceptive to procedures.

programme.title: Surgeons: A Matter of Life or Death
programme.desc: Abi, who battles facial pain so severe that it leaves her rocking on the floor, undergoes a hugely risky procedure to detangle a tumour from a nerve in her brain.

programme.title: Skin A&E
programme.desc: Following work at a specialist clinic where four top dermatologists treat a range of conditions. They squeeze, drain or pop cysts, lipomas and anything else that's getting under their patients' skin.

programme.title: Accused: Guilty or Innocent?
programme.desc: Late night in the Bronx, an off-duty Correction Officer believes he's been shot at by the occupants of a vehicle. He returns fire, killing a teenage passenger. A 'Gel Bead Blaster' is found in the vehicle.

programme.sub-title: The One With All the Kissing
programme.desc: Ross's new wife won't speak to him, Rachel appoints Monica her adviser in romance, and Phoebe feels neglected.

programme.desc: Join Draco and his friend the yellow teddy bear as the two have lots of fun playing and swinging together on the seesaw, learning about opposites as they take turns going up and down.

programme.desc: Join Gaya the Fairy as she enjoys the warm and nice spring, the meadows, and the yellow flowers that wither and grow little tufts of white hairs just like grandfathers, only to blow away in the wind.

programme.desc: Documenting the place of village halls at the heart of communities across Scotland throughout the 20th century. The tearing down of old village halls across the Highlands and Islands marks the end of an era. Balivanich, Carinish, Acharacle, Lochmaddy, Leverburgh, Shawbost, Vatersay, Aird, Ness, the Highlanders' Institute - many halls have been replaced by buildings which focus their attention on a different generation.

programme.title: Anna Haugh's Big Irish Food Tour
programme.desc: The 1985 Black Ball Final made snooker legend Dennis Taylor a household name, and there's nowhere that celebrates this giant of sport more than his native Tyrone. Anna visits him at the snooker hall where it all began. Dennis can still remember the soda bread his grandmother used to make over an open fire. programme.title: Anna Haugh's Big Irish Food Tour
programme.desc: The 1985 Black Ball Final made snooker legend Dennis Taylor a household name, and there's nowhere that celebrates this giant of sport more than his native Tyrone. Anna visits him at the snooker hall where it all began. Dennis can still remember the soda bread his grandmother used to make over an open fire. Can Anna recreate this childhood recipe?The pair then join fishermen on the largest lake in the UK to celebrate one of Ireland's most unique delicacies - the Lough Neagh eel.

programme.title: Reporting Scotland
programme.desc: The latest news and weather from around Scotland.

programme.title: Serie A Scots
programme.desc: Billy Gilmour and Scott McTominay had an incredible first season in Italian football. Their Napoli side won the League and Super Cup, but perhaps their greatest achievement is yet to come with Scotland heading to World Cup 2026. Jonathan Sutherland returns to Napoli to meet the Scottish superstars of Serie A.

programme.title: Away with the Tartan Army: Scotland's Best Moments
programme.desc: Featuring rare archive and home video, Stuart Cosgrove and Tam Cowan explore the highs and lows of following the Scotland international men's team, win, lose or draw, and take a trip with the Tartan Army to count down Scotland's best moments.

programme.title: Pitch Invasion: How the Scottish and Irish Changed Football
programme.desc: In the early 1970s, Leeds United was the dominant force in English football. Central to this success was their tough tackling midfield led by Irishman Johnny Giles and their Scottish captain Billy Bremner.

programme.title: Still Game
programme.desc: While Isa gets into a fix with some cute little animals, Jack and Victor decide to stop putting things off and go on that adventure they have been planning for years. A new month rolls round and Jack is excited to see what new picture he will reveal on his calendar, but tensions quickly rise as Victor tries to meddle in his fun.

programme.title: Half Man
programme.desc: 2010. Niall and Ava face major life changes as Ruben struggles with his relationship. An impulsive choice between Mona and Niall has serious consequences for all involved.

programme.title: Two Doors Down
programme.desc: Beth and Cathy think they are doing their good deed for the day when they drop Christine off at the hospital reception, but while Cathy manages to slip away, poor Beth doesn't get off so lightly and is committed to a visit post-op. With a long list of demands from Christine, Beth and Eric return later with her supplies to find that instead of being sore and exhausted after surgery, she has been sleeping off her dinner.

programme.title: Reporting Scotland
programme.desc: The latest news and weather from around Scotland.

programme.title: The Repair Shop: On the Road
programme.desc: Will and Dom learn how a devastating fire put a 5,000-year-old craft in jeopardy, Chris Shaw repairs a Gaelic pipe music book, and Dom has the chance to make his own sporran.

programme.title: Icons of Football
programme.desc: A portrait of Julie Fleeting, who became Scotland's all-time top scorer and an Arsenal legend while balancing life as a mum and PE teacher.

programme.title: Highland Cops
programme.desc: Police are alerted to the discovery of human remains near Aviemore. They suspect this might be the last resting place of Rodrigo Falcon, a popular barman who went missing in December 2022. Sergeant Stephen Todd and his team confront a woman who is keeping monkeys and tropical animals in a ruined house in freezing cold Caithness. In Inverness, PC Siobhan Cooper faces rowdy bank holiday crowds.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Ken forces Richard to accompany him to Aviemore so that he can help him navigate the QR code scanner at the train station ticket barrier. However, a mix-up on the platform turns what should be a simple day trip into an almighty fiasco.

programme.title: Pitch Invasion: How the Scottish and Irish Changed Football
programme.desc: How managers and players like David Moyes and Charlie Nicholas dealt with the highs and lows of English football. While Charlie Nicholas made headlines off the pitch in the 1980s, David Moyes saw glory turn to failure in 2014.

programme.title: TRNSMT
programme.desc: Shereen Cutkelvin presents highlights from TRNSMT 2025, including performances from Wet Leg, James Bay, Jake Bugg, Fontaines DC, Jade, Biffy Clyro and Snow Patrol.

programme.title: The Catch-Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: 24 Hours with Ella Toone
programme.desc: Unprecedented behind-the-scenes access to Manchester United and England star Ella Toone as she prepares for her wedding, while still coming to terms with the loss of her biggest fan, her dad.

programme.title: Shrek
programme.desc: The story of a reclusive green ogre called Shrek whose life of solitude is disrupted by a horde of refugees who have been banished by the evil Lord Farquaad. Determined to save his home, Shrek cuts a deal with the tyrant, who sets him a daunting task - if Shrek is ever to enjoy peace and quiet again, he must rescue Farquaad's bride-to-be from a dangerous castle.

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The team looks forward to welcoming a new recruit into their midst, whilst Mervin can't wait to get off the island. But before he catches his flight, Mervin becomes embroiled in the murder of a young man who's found in a ravine. The case becomes more compelling when they find the victim has left them a mysterious message.

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: It's that time of year again - the Mr Teesside Beauty Pageant, and the grand prize is £20,000, but someone foul lurks in the shadows to attempt to fix the competition.

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Dickie's been booked for a drag gig and has invited a load of agents in the hope of making it to the big time, while Mam faces a ghost from her past.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip attempts to return to normal life and focus on her friends, school and Ravi. But when a key witness in Max Hastings's trial disappears, Pip feels the pull of a new case.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip hits a dead end and rethinks. Digging into Jamie's recent behaviour and last movements, she realises he may not be an innocent victim after all.

programme.title: Paranormal: The Girl, the Ghost and the Gravestones
programme.desc: Sian Eleri investigates a seemingly ordinary farmhouse in north Wales, which, in the late 1990s, was widely considered the most haunted house in Britain. The story made local and national headlines as the family who lived at the farm became ever more desperate in their search for answers. More than 300 allegedly paranormal events were documented at the Flintshire farmhouse in video footage, audio interviews, photos and eyewitness accounts - all of which has been locked away until now.

programme.title: Paranormal: The Girl, the Ghost and the Gravestones
programme.desc: Sian is frustrated that she still hasn't been able to contact the Gower family who lived at the farmhouse - David, a former headmaster, his wife Rose-Mary and their son John-Paul. But there is a breakthrough of another kind when the current owner of Penyffordd Farm gets in touch and invites her over to see the house she's heard so much about. Sian is welcomed in by Michael, who bought the house in Flintshire from the Gowers despite stories that it was haunted.

programme.title: Paranormal: The Girl, the Ghost and the Gravestones
programme.desc: Sian Eleri's investigation into the haunting of Penyffordd Farm continues as multiple witnesses claim to have seen a hooded figure. She wonders whether they could really have all imagined the same thing.

programme.title: Paranormal: The Girl, the Ghost and the Gravestones
programme.desc: Sian's investigation into an allegedly haunted house in North Wales concludes. She must track down the last family members and solve whether this really was paranormal or all a hoax.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Sports mockumentary about GB Paralympian Darren Silverstone, who stole the hearts of a nation before his infamous chokey-o in Tokyo. For the first time, he tells his story - and starts his glorious comeback. In Tokyo 2020, Darren Silverstone was the Paras GB poster boy. (He was on yoghurts, too.) He cruised to the final and charmed the nation along the way with his signature dance move, the dazzle.

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: It's that time of year again - the Mr Teesside Beauty Pageant, and the grand prize is £20,000, but someone foul lurks in the shadows to attempt to fix the competition.

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Dickie's been booked for a drag gig and has invited a load of agents in the hope of making it to the big time, while Mam faces a ghost from her past.

programme.title: This Is BBC TWO
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion's in Sheffield, where he feels there is scope to double the size of a property. He is also in Nottingham with layout changes on the menu. Jacqui offers a 16th-century history lesson in Windsor.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Continuing his travels in Budapest, Michael Portillo begins in the small town of Huvösvölgy, where he encounters a surprising railway run almost entirely by children.

programme.title: Hidden Treasures of the National Trust
programme.sub-title: The Duke, His Wife and Their Lover
programme.desc: From scandalous society portraits to glittering royal furniture and the restoration of lost landscapes, this episode reveals how the legacy of the Grand Tour continues to shape Britain's great houses - and the work being done today to protect their remarkable stories for the future.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Political Thinking with Nick Robinson
programme.desc: Nick Robinson has a conversation with, not an interrogation of, the people who shape our political thinking about what shaped theirs.

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Ben Fogle presents an appeal on behalf of Hearing Dogs for Deaf People, a charity that provides support as well as trained assistance dogs to help those living with deafness or hearing loss in the UK.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Thirty players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie presents another heat of the classic quiz. The specialist subjects are the British Industrial Revolution, the films of Christopher Guest, Charles Rennie Mackintosh and Succession.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio.

programme.title: Glorious Gardens From Above
programme.desc: Christine Walkden visits Scampston Hall in Yorkshire and catches up with an old friend at York Gate Garden.

programme.title: The Secret Life of Elephants
programme.desc: The rains have arrived after the drought, but has baby elephant Breeze survived? As hundreds of elephants descend on the reserve for the breeding season, the research team hit their busiest time of year. Saba Douglas-Hamilton tracks down the biggest land animals on Earth, the mighty bull elephants, but even Saba is keeping her distance when Rommel, a dangerous bull, turns up.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel is in Chichester Cathedral with silver expert Alastair Dickenson, who finds a fine cow creamer and a rare freedom box, while expert Clive Farahar is shown a wonderful autograph album.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Crowcombe Court in West Somerset. Antiques experts James Lewis and Thomas Plant uncover sausage plates that were designed to promote the wares of a Victorian butcher, and Paul rides on the footplate of a steam locomotive.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Cheryl Baker, Chris Bavin, Axel Blake and Sophie Willan take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: At Budapest's historic Nyugati station, Michael Portillo explores its sumptuous royal waiting room, built specially for the Austro-Hungarian emperor Franz Joseph I and his empress, Elizabeth.

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: The wait is over: the search for Northern Ireland's greatest garden is almost at an end. Five passionate gardeners have reached the final. After an early autumn road trip spent revisiting these five truly incredible gardens to refresh their memories and see how the gardens are faring, Carol and Diarmuid join the finalists, their families and friends at Greenmount's walled garden in County Antrim to reveal which one of the five will be named Greatest Garden.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Some more unusual growing methods are demonstrated at Beechgrove Garden, with Ruth Vichos and Brian Cunningham attempting to produce a mushroom crop using a kit.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With summer just around the corner, Monty is bringing bold colour to the garden, planting gingers and cannas, setting out tomatoes and sowing wallflowers straight into the soil.

programme.title: Hidden Treasures of the National Trust
programme.desc: Preserving the legacy of St Cuthbert and a colony of breeding seabirds, on the remote Farne Islands. And in Wales, experts race to save a fragile bible that shaped Welsh culture.

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul, Bob and Ted travel a short distance to Thornwood Springs Fishery in Essex, a complex of three lakes on the edges of Epping Forest, in search of a big rudd - although there's also the chance of a trout in the waters.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Featuring extensive coverage of the day's top national and international news stories, along with explanations about the context and potential aftereffects.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Love & Mercy
programme.desc: In the late 1960s, the Beach Boys' Brian Wilson stops touring, produces `Pet Sounds' and begins to lose his grip on reality. By the 1980s, Wilson, under the sway of a controlling therapist, finds a saviour in Melinda Ledbetter.

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Knockout week is coming to an end, but not before a few more cooks have to say goodbye. Ten contestants remain, and there is only room in the semi-finals for eight.

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay puts Nitin Ganatra, Rev Kate Bottley and Gladiator Apollo's UK knowledge to the test, with the help of unreliable tour guide Paul Gorton. Today's destination is Dudley.

programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: On an ill-fated group boat trip, Zoe tries to pretend last night's betrayal didn't mean anything. But Dan's behaviour threatens to push Zoe over the edge. Nat uses the kiss to drive a wedge between Jacob and his boyfriend Will, who has shown up uninvited. Solomon is the only one who doesn't know what happened last night - does Zoe tell him and destroy two marriages? Jess is forced to take drastic action to avoid confrontation.

programme.desc: A rare guitar finds its way into the shop and Ashley faces off with a customer.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Seth pushes for forgiveness when Rich commits an offence at the jewellery counter.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: When customers start getting scammed in his parking lot, the Golds have to take action.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team are in Burton-on-Trent, where some interesting finds include a Victorian amber bracelet, a Moorcroft vase and a blunderbuss, but the highlight of the day is a bronze pheasant that lands on Tim Hogarth's table.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his team of dealers are in Ilkeston in Derbyshire where an art deco clock gets Tony Geering in a poetic mood, and Karen Dalmeny confesses to a youthful postcard addiction. Alison Chapman and Simon Schneider are also in the team today.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: The guys are thrilled when a $1 bill signed by Clark Gable arrives. An item from Ulysses S. Grant's funeral also finds its way into Rick's hands.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: A model train once owned by Johnny Cash comes into the shop. Other items include a 1920's chicken incubator and a 17th century Tibetan Buddha.

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah's on a mission to rescue unloved and unwanted items from Witley Recycling Centre. With three eclectic tip-finds to transform, Sarah turns to Neil Wragg to come up with a creative new use for an old kite surfing sail while the Bristol Upholstery Collective is tasked with turning a forlorn futon into something fabulous.

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly
programme.desc: Henry Cole and top restorers Allen Millyard and Guy Willison scour the country, challenging themselves to make money out of buying and selling other people's automotive junk and classic collectibles.

programme.title: Storage Wars Texas
programme.desc: The bidders seek treasures in the upscale hamlet of Haslet, Ricky's mother, Puffy, clashes with Lesa, Jenny and Mary take gambles, Ricky ventures where men fear to tread.

programme.title: Storage Wars Texas
programme.sub-title: For the Benefit of Mr. Charles
programme.desc: Jenny employs a new strategy when the buyers arrive in the city of Longview, Matt Blevins tries scare tactics and Mary's old friend keeps the bidders guessing.

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Modern imaging technology and analysis from military and intelligence professionals, as well as leading scientists, investigate mysteries from strange geological occurrences to odd man-made structures as seen by more than 4,000 orbiting satellites.

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Six breathtaking images, six unfathomable mysteries. From a gigantic tsunami that never hits land to a sinister blood-red lake in war-torn Baghdad.

programme.title: Cursed Treasures
programme.desc: The hunt leads to Anglo-Saxon England, medieval Europe, China under its first Emperor and Egypt when the Pharaohs still ruled, where the origins of the curses surrounding the Staffordshire Hoard, Black Prince's Ruby, and more are uncovered.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: The guys question the value of a can of joke elephant manure and check out some signed presidential memoirs and a robot toy from the movie Forbidden Planet.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: One of the rarest guns in American history pops up in the Pawn Stars' shop, as well as a tyre that once belonged to NASCAR driver Dale Earnhardt.

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Upcycler Jay Blades is at Altrincham Recycling Centre, on a mission to save three unwanted items from the skip. To help convert his trash into cash, Jay turns to metal maestro Kev Paxton to revive a set of glass lampshades. Meanwhile, wallpapering wonder Emma Walker has a big job on her hands with an oak sideboard in need of an update.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Throughout the course of human history, some caves have been considered sacred places for spiritual encounter and enlightenment, while others have been feared as true portals to purgatory.

programme.title: UFOs: The Lost Evidence
programme.desc: Native American myths and UFOs have been linked for thousands of years. Evidence and modern-day sightings cause experts to revisit these legends.

Also on the team is Karen Dalemany.

programme.title: WWE's Greatest Moments
programme.desc: In the 80s and 90s, Saturday Night's Main Event was appointment television for every WWE fan. Breaking ground as the first professional wrestling programme to air on network television, Saturday Night's Main Event was home.

programme.title: WWE's Greatest Moments
programme.desc: Otherwise, known as Cactus Jack, Mankind and Dude Love, Mick Foley is one of the most beloved Superstars of all time.

programme.title: The UnBelievable With Dan Aykroyd
programme.desc: A look at tales of strange things below Earth's surface, including sea beasts, hidden cities, and tunnels carved by giant sloths.

programme.title: Paranormal Caught on Camera
programme.sub-title: Penitentiary Phantoms in Tennessee and More
programme.desc: Experts analyse footage from paranormal videos, specialists provide insights, while eyewitnesses give firsthand accounts of strange phenomena.

programme.title: Paranormal Survivor
programme.desc: A couple unleashes something terrifying while renovating a classic car; a pair of murderous spirits possess a family in Pennsylvania; something demonic takes over a teenage boy, forcing his parents to take drastic action.

programme.sub-title: Top of the Pups: Row Your Boat
programme.desc: Mr an Mr Crab sing 'Row Your Boat' as they take a ride down the river together.

programme.title: Playtime Towers
programme.desc: Bella and Lift have fun playing with their favourite red bouncy ball. It bounces into the Ice Disco, where it gives Hettie the Yeti an idea for a bouncy dance!

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: During Bing's bedtime routine, he inadvertently pees on his blankie, and it's too wet to wash and dry before bed. Fortunately, his Hoppity Voosh toy has a cape just as soft and huggy as a blankie, and it even smells just right too.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say `yo-yo' and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see all sorts of yo-yos: some spotty yo-yos, a stripy yo-yo, an up and down yo-yo, a rocking yo-yo and even a peepo yo-yo.

programme.title: Lu & the Bally Bunch
programme.desc: Lu wants her dad to play with her while he's working from home.

programme.title: Kiri and Lou
programme.desc: The friends are all playing hide and seek. Lou is the seeker, but he falls asleep before he can count to ten.

programme.title: Froglets
programme.desc: The Froglets tackle a tricky obstacle course, but can they climb a mountain of cushions?

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos get carried away when they roll a picnic down a hill. Back in the Boat, the Very Important Lady falls apart and her head rolls back and forth, thwarting the Twirlywoos' attempts to put her back together!

programme.title: Something Special
programme.sub-title: We're All Friends: Get Ready Go
programme.desc: Maddie and friends get ready for the day, while sorting out toys makes Mr Tumble sleepy.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky finds a party invitation and shows it to Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po. The invitation takes flight and the Teletubbies follow it to find a Tiddlytubbies' party.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear welcome babies Noah, Baani, Zachary, Amber, Amelia, Jace, Hunter and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their babies at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside everyone's bags are toy animals which are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding baby appropriate toy animals inside a fabric bag or pillowcase.

programme.title: Tiny Tunes
programme.sub-title: Hush Little Baby Don't You Cry
programme.desc: Lisa and the children make mobiles and sign and sing 'Hush Little Baby Don't You Cry'.

programme.title: I Can Grow It
programme.desc: Springtime is in full flow, and there are lots of different plants and flowers popping up in the city garden. Nelly and Isla are teaming up with Aaron to see what amazing plants they can find, and they've brought their teddies along for the journey too.

programme.title: Monster Makes
programme.desc: Rebecca washes all the hats, ready for a silly hat party - but Murphy's special party hat has shrunk. Although he's disappointed, it gives Murphy an idea - a clothes peg giraffe. The Mini Monsters are using everyday things to create great makes, and a call comes in on the Magic Monster Mirror from Rebecca's aunt. Murphy's mega monster make is a fun woolly head game, inspired by scraps of wool delivered by the Recycling Express.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Minty soars over the valley after being flung onto the back of a baby Bokchoydactyl.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey, Bingo and Missy take to the high seas and play pirates on the hammock swing, but they'll need to gather all their courage to take on storms and whale attacks.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are confused when they see Duggee sitting on a ball because they like to use it differently. Duggee is quick to explain to the Squirrels that there is no right or wrong way to play and that everyone is different.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee is a big, friendly dog who runs The Squirrel Club, an after-school activity club where children can learn and take part in all kinds of activities, have fun adventures, and earn badges for their achievements.

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk through the town to the florist. On the way they spot a treasure chest, listen to the raindrops on the pond, discover who might live in a hedge, and even meet a square spider!

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: It's Mummy's birthday. Henry, Sophie and Mama go on a secret shopping trip to find the perfect present. Back at home, Henry plans out a treasure map and sends Mummy on a heroic birthday adventure.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Vinnie's family is English and they come from a city called Bristol, which is located in the west of England. Bristol is famous for hot-air balloons and pirates! Vinnie makes his tarts by mixing the leaves from parsley and thyme into cream cheese.

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.sub-title: Cricket Bat and Wind Up Toy
programme.desc: Maddie is finding out about wind-up toys and how they move along. We see inside a wind-up truck and Maddie uses her special cameras to show us how the spring inside gets tighter and tighter and stores energy so that when it is released the cogs inside move the wheels. Next, Maddie visits a factory where cricket bats are made from willow trees, because it's bouncy and strong.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Rayane, Karim and Luca are having a sleepover, when hearing dog Diesel is woken in the middle of the night by a strange hissing noise. Is there a slithery snake in the kitchen?

programme.title: Go Wild
programme.sub-title: Aneeshwar and Lizzie: Pygmy Hippo and Aardvark
programme.desc: Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet a pygmy hippo and some aardvarks.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle, Jess, Gramps and Angela help Pepper find a new owner by making a leaflet. Waffle tries to help Mrs Hobbs, too, but causes chaos. Waffle hopes Mrs Hobbs will adopt Pepper.

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: Bernie is keen to have a quiet day on his own, but he has a surprise visitor; Bernadette, his lady friend, is coming to shore.

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: Animated series about a group of potatoes who sing songs in different genres. In this blues song, the Potatoes enter famous paintings and sculptures, celebrating different ways art is created.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: As everyone huddles in Sonny's caravan during a storm, Toad admits he's frightened of the sound. Can Yukee find a way to use the pitter-pitter-pitter-patter of the rain to calm him?

programme.title: Maddie and Triggs
programme.desc: Maddie is disheartened to find her sunflower has not grown as tall as others. What might be preventing this little flower from thriving?

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels have just got some new walkie-talkies and are having so much fun with them. Duggee calls the Squirrels to come for some blueberry juice, but as Tag is running, he trips over a car and falls through a secret door in the wall. Communicating through his walkie-talkie, Tag doesn't know where he is. He can see lots and lots of things around him, but no-one has any idea where this room is in the house.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: It's a rainy day for a picnic, but when lucky number Seven appears, everything comes up rainbows. Learn all about the number seven.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: The adventures of 26 lively letters who can make words come to life by holding hands. O has fun doubling up and making new and different oo sounds in the words moon, foot and boot. He even gets to hoot like an owl.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.sub-title: We're All Friends: How's the Weather?
programme.desc: Justin and friends enjoy ice cream weather, while Mr Tumble dodges a rain shower.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies bottom bounce on The Tubby Bouncy before wiggling their bottoms to a special bottom bump song.

programme.title: Ranger Hamza's Eco Quest
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers discover how allotments play an important role in the environment. They find out why they are great places to grow fruit and vegetables, explore a compost heap where worms and birds live and spot a water butt which recycles rainwater. On the way, they enjoy the peace and quiet of the allotment, before planting some potatoes.

programme.title: Football Fantastics
programme.desc: Who is going to win the trophy for the match between the Fantastics and Grown Ups United?

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Grace explains the prominent features of three models of motorbike and demonstrates how to ride them.

programme.title: Dodge's Pup School
programme.desc: Miss Collie challenges the pups to build a raft to carry a dog biscuit across a paddling pool. Woofer and Jazz both have great ideas for building the raft, but Dodge can't choose whose idea is better.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger the Tricarrotops explores the world of the crunchiest era, going on adventures throughout the colourful Vegesaur Valley.

programme.title: Octonauts: Above & Beyond
programme.desc: Periwinkle strays during a Bialowieza forest mission and discovers a rare European bison.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: When Peter promises the Shrew some worms to entice him to Cotton-tail's birthday party, he ends up accidentally putting everyone in danger.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee thought his tie was a little bit plain, so he added some accessories and now it looks amazing. The Squirrels admire Duggee's new look and want to learn how to accessorise themselves. The first step for the Squirrels is to find something they'd like to change. The Squirrels each bring a different item: wellies, a T-shirt, headband, helmet and a potato. They grab sequins, patches, feathers and lights, and then showcase what they have all done.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: Tidbit practises so much for his hiking badge that he gives himself blisters.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Evil Pea takes all the books from the supermarket. Supertato, Chilli and Carrot try to rescue them by using acrobatic skills that they learnt from their favourite circus book.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Felix the pig is stuck on a cliff ledge where not even Mojo's tops can reach him. They'll need to be creative to get Bo up there and save that poor pig!

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo hide under the house and spy on two mysterious tradies working in the backyard.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Mum and Dad tell Bluey and Bingo that they're dropping them off at Nana's so they can have some time to themselves. The girls are outraged and imagine an elaborate plan to infiltrate their parent's private time.

programme.title: Zog
programme.desc: The doctors visit Fifi and see that she is feeling sad because her pies aren't up to her usual standard. They take her out to cheer her up - until Zog realises what she really needs.

programme.title: Balamory
programme.desc: Miss Hoolie is planning a cycling holiday around Balamory, but her old bike needs serious TLC. Ava offers to fix it, but a few small repairs turn into a much bigger challenge.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: It's the morning of Evie's first day at school and the Brooklyn-Bells are on schedule. But Waffle isn't happy. He's expecting his usual morning walkies. Doug tells Waffle that Gramps will be walking him today, he'll be coming to the house later. Waffle is insistent, he wants to walk to school with the rest of the family.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: A wayward camping trip teaches Rafi that changing the plan can lead to adventure.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.sub-title: - Spring:It's Time to Fly a Kite
programme.desc: On a windy day, JoJo and Gran Gran take Gran Gran's old kite to fly in the park. After a strong gust of wind, the kite flies away and rips! JoJo makes a design that Gran Gran sews on the kite to repair it. JoJo learns that if something breaks, it can sometimes be repaired.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: A game of hide and seek takes longer than usual when Duck gets scared by some fireworks.

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: When Tiny is out fishing in the music boat, she catches four strange metal objects. They are a bit like hoots but much smaller. She takes them home to show Small, but when Small arrives, the metal things have gone.

programme.title: Beddybyes
programme.desc: MeMo and BaBa want to spin without stopping but learn that it's good to take breaks.

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Upsy Daisy and Igglepiggle are out in the garden together. They find the ball, and soon everyone is kicking it to each other. Upsy Daisy kicks the ball again and it heads straight for the Pontipines house.

programme.title: Cbeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: A different story read by a special guest each night just before bedtime.

programme.title: 40 Huge Floorfillers of the 2020s
programme.desc: 40 blistering tracks from 2020 to 2025. With Calvin, Guetta, Doja, Gaga and so many more awesome tunes!

programme.title: Future Classics! The 2020s
programme.desc: No excuses, stop what you are doing, turn it up and party hard with the biggest anthems and future classics of the twenty twenties!

programme.title: Evolution of Dance! - 86' to 26'
programme.desc: Starting in with the vinyl hits of 80s, the CD singles of the 90s, The downloads of the 2000s and finishing with today streaming hits. This is the evolution of dance!

programme.title: Clubland Smashed! In the Mix
programme.desc: We've raided the Clubland vaults and decided to smash things up a bit! Enjoy some of the most awesome classics there are, mixed and smashed up to perfection.

programme.title: Euphoric Clubland: Smashed
programme.desc: Created by us for you! Clubland takes you deep in the mix with the biggest euphoric anthems ever.

programme.title: 100% Euphoria! 1995 to 1999
programme.desc: The latter part of the 90s witnessed the euphoric revolution. With massive `hands in the air' anthems from Energy 52, Chicane, PVD, Ayla and loads more, this is 100% euphoria!

programme.title: Infinite Euphoria!
programme.desc: Dive into a world of uplifting melodies, driving beats and breakdowns to give you shivers! This is Euphoria at its finest.

programme.title: 100% Euphoria! 2000-2005
programme.desc: Trance and its euphoric sound took over in the early 2000s. The clubs, charts and airwaves were full Angelic, Fragma, iiO, Ian van Dahl and more! This is 100% Euphoria!

programme.title: 50 Ultimate Rave Anthems!
programme.desc: The period from the late 80s to early 90s: the authorities hated it, the media attacked it, but the people loved it! Clubland brings you `The Glow Stick Bangers'!

programme.title: I Can't Get No Sleep!
programme.desc: Fancy a mash up after midnight? Only massive, ad free anthems to help with your insomnia! More beats, less sleep!

programme.desc: Mr Bean buys a portable television but can't get it to work properly. Then, when his camera is stolen, he must identify the suspect in a police line up. Afterwards, he attends a magic show and disco with his girlfriend but ends up embarrassing her.

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Mr Bean goes to a swimming pool but finds himself terrified of the high diving board; afterwards, he must try several imaginative ways to get out of the car park when he doesn't have enough money to pay. He then demonstrates the proper way to make a sandwich whilst out at the park. Finally, when Mr Bean and his girlfriend go on a date to watch a horror movie he teases her for being scared, until the film actually starts.

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: Featuring music performances from chart sensation Olly Murs, 80s comeback king Rick Astley and stand-up comedy from Irish rising star Al Porter. Also, double gold medal winning swimmer Rebecca Adlington hands over her phone in celebrity Send to All.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Can't Remember Which Sister
programme.desc: Ross is suspicious about Rachel's possible job offer from a good-looking stranger, while Chandler can't remember which of Joey's many attractive sisters he kissed at a party.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With All the Jealousy
programme.desc: Ross becomes increasingly jealous of Rachel's sexy new colleague, Mark, and gets the wrong end of the stick when he spies on Rachel at her office. Meanwhile, Monica is enamoured with a sexy busboy who writes love poems for her, and Joey gets himself into trouble by faking his CV in order to get a part in a Broadway musical.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Monica and Richard Are Just Friends
programme.desc: Monica bumps into Richard in a video store and they decide to work on their friendship, but the more time they spend together, the more sexual their relationship becomes. Meanwhile, Phoebe dates an athlete who unintentionally keeps exposing himself by wearing loose shorts, and Rachel agrees to read `The Shining' if Joey will read `Little Women'.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe is reunited with her former singing partner, Leslie, a failed television commercial jingle writer who wants to use Phoebe's signature song `Smelly Cat' in an advertisement for cat litter. Meanwhile, Ross becomes jealous when Rachel agrees to go to a fashion lecture with Mark, a former colleague who has just left for a new job, and Chandler meets a beautiful woman, Ginger, who has a prosthetic leg.

programme.title: Jimmy Carr's Am I the A******?
programme.sub-title: Love Lies and a Buffet Surprise
programme.desc: ...A******? Jimmy Carr, GK Barry & Jamali Maddix put pettiness under the microscope, incl. a woman who lied about her age to a partner and a mum's extreme response to her daughter's mess.

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: Joining Michael is global superstar Camila Cabello who will be singing her single `Easy' and former King of the Jungle and football legend Harry Redknapp who hands over his phone to play Send to All. Michael also wakes up one of the biggest boy bands in the world for an extra special Midnight Gameshow with Westlife, plus there are incredible surprises in store for members of the audience.

programme.desc: Phoebe's lack of coordination while jogging embarrasses Rachel, and Joey gets a sexy new roommate.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray is excited about meeting a distant relative, until she begins to overstay her welcome.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Worrying that Ray only visits her to get a good meal, Marie hesitates to teach Debra her cooking secrets.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Raymond takes over the family finances to prove to Debra how easy it is, but he soon discovers that he's no accountant.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Raymond gains new respect for his brother when he sees him risk his life on the job.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray and Debra can't get a good night's sleep when Ally fears there are monsters in her room.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe invites everyone to stay at her massage client's beach house for the weekend. Phoebe meets someone who knows details about her family, Rachel expresses her jealousy of Ross' uninhibited girlfriend, and Joey nags the friends into agreeing to play a party game that requires the participants to gradually take off items of clothing.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross is dating Rachel again but hates what he had to do to get back together, while Phoebe and her real mother, Phoebe Snr, get to know each other better. Meanwhile, Monica is badly stung by a jellyfish and has to resort to drastic measures to relieve the pain.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandler and Joey lose all their furniture, Monica finds that one date with her high-school heartthrob is more than enough, and Ross plays tricks on Rachel following their break-up. Elsewhere, Phoebe is temporarily convinced that a cat is the reincarnation of her dead adoptive mother Lily.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandler has a brief fling with Rachel's boss Joanna which leaves him handcuffed without his trousers in her office. Meanwhile, Joey can only afford one book in an encyclopaedia set, and Monica caters a party for her mother.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Ballroom Dancing
programme.desc: Joey argues with the building superintendent Treegar and ends up having to help him practise ballroom dancing. Meanwhile, Ross and Chandler can't get out of their gym membership even though neither of them wants to exercise, and Phoebe loses her massage job for kissing a client.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Joey's New Girlfriend
programme.desc: Chandler falls for Joey's new date, Ross discovers that the woman that he thinks he's dating actually thinks he's the babysitter, Rachel's new college boyfriend steals, and Phoebe tries to re-catch a cold to improve her singing voice.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Dirty Girl
programme.desc: Phoebe and Monica decide to go into business together after they cater a funeral where the widow tries not to pay, and Ross' date with the beautiful Cheryl goes awry when he kills a rat in her filthy apartment.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Crosses the Line
programme.desc: Chandler finally confesses to his feelings for Kathy just after she splits up with Joey, Ross rediscovers his hopeless musical skills on the keyboard which Phoebe alone appreciates, and Chandler buys new furniture for the apartment since the robbery left them with just the canoe, chick and duck.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Chandler in a Box
programme.desc: The friends have a Thanksgiving meal together, Monica is reminded of Richard when she tries to kiss his son, and Joey frees Chandler from the box that he's being kept in to stop Kathy leaving him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where They're Going to Party!
programme.desc: Monica upsets Phoebe by stopping the catering business before it has started after she gets the head chef job at a restaurant where the staff all hate her, and Rachel's boss is killed in a car accident before she can make Rachel a junior buyer.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Girl From Poughkeepsie
programme.desc: Ross struggles to maintain a long-distance relationship, Chandler's well-intended attempts to get Rachel a date seem to miss the mark, and Monica's plan to assert some authority at her new restaurant goes awry.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe's half-brother makes an astounding announcement and a thought-provoking request, while Joey finds himself in a class struggle.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe undergoes an embryo implant procedure to become a surrogate mother for her brother and his wife, and Monica and Rachel wager their apartment in a trivia game against Chandler and Joey.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel schemes to get a handsome client to ask her on a date, while Chandler's jealousy causes problems with his girlfriend. Elsewhere, after losing a bet, Monica and Rachel must swap their apartment with Chandler and Joey's.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Joey's Dirty Day
programme.desc: Charlton Heston finds Joey in a compromising position during a movie shoot. Meanwhile, Rachel regrets asking Ross for a favour, as he ends up spending the weekend in a Vermont cabin with Emily.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With All the Rugby
programme.desc: Ross takes up rugby to impress Emily, and Chandler sets up a ruse to avoid his former girlfriend Janice.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Got High
programme.desc: On Thanksgiving, Chandler tries to gain favour with Monica's parents Jack and Judy, but an old secret involving Ross causes problems that he is unaware of. Meanwhile, Rachel proves she is no domestic goddess when she creates a quite disastrous dessert.

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: A 12-year-old paperboy's disappearance during his Sunday morning route shocks a peaceful Iowa city. Panic sets in when a second paperboy goes missing.

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.desc: When homesick university student Miya Marcano books a flight to South Florida for the weekend, her family is thrilled. However, when the plane lands, Miya is nowhere to be found.

programme.title: The First 48
programme.desc: When an Atlanta father of two is gunned down with an AK-47, rookie detective Scott Berhalter must sift through lies and alibis to get his killers off the street.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter
programme.desc: When a woman fails to come home from work, her daughter knows something terrible must have happened and reports her missing; as the investigation unfolds, Lt Kenda realises he could be dealing with a fledgling serial killer.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter
programme.desc: A gang member, a jealous lover or a complete stranger? Kenda and Anderson hunt for a killer who shot a young man outside his home.

programme.title: Fugitives Caught on Tape
programme.desc: A wanted felon smashes into a state trooper's car while fleeing from local police and county deputies; police get into a shootout with a man wanted for murdering his roommate; a deputy's PIT manoeuvre knocks a fugitive's car up on two wheels.

programme.title: My Strange Arrest
programme.desc: A man calls 911 on the cops, a woman is accused of stealing a horse and buggy, a man allegedly trespasses and shoots rats in a neighbour's yard, and more.

programme.title: My Strange Arrest
programme.desc: A look at some of the strangest arrests from across the country.

programme.title: Court Cam
programme.desc: The judge has harsh words for a man convicted of killing his mother and her boyfriend; a woman charged with Assault and Battery takes out her frustrations on a jailhouse podium; the judge mutes a defendant who won't stop interrupting him in court.

programme.title: Murder Detective
programme.desc: New. Sharon Birchwood: Former senior detective Graham Hill sheds new light on the inner workings, twists and turns of criminal investigations, showing how they were solved. (S1, ep 1)

programme.title: Murder Detective
programme.desc: In April 2019, the remains of a woman were found in a freezer in an apartment in London's East End. Investigators brought the freezer to be x-rayed, and it revealed two frozen bodies.

programme.title: Murder Detective
programme.desc: Maheswaran Kaneshan sought asylum in the UK, but became caught up in a gang feud that turned deadly. His life was controlled by the gang that had brought him to London. Gang bosses brutally beat Maheswaran, causing him to later die in hospital.

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.sub-title: Evil Unearthed On A Sleepy Farm
programme.desc: A farmhouse in a quaint, wooded town became one of New Hampshire's most notorious crime scenes. Delusional killings confounded investigators and appalled residents of Epping, New Hampshire.

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy
programme.sub-title: The Killings that Shook a College Community
programme.desc: A young boy and his family's housekeeper are brutally murdered in an upscale Omaha neighbourhood. Their case remains unsolved for years until another double homicide reveals a link and unmasks a serial killer.

programme.title: Accident, Suicide, or Murder
programme.desc: A beautiful young woman is found in her locked apartment with 20 stab wounds. The medical examiner classifies her death as murder but later changes the ruling to suicide, a decision that forces her family into the fight of their lives.

programme.title: Accident, Suicide, or Murder
programme.desc: A terrifying crash off a mountain road leaves one woman dead and her husband injured. The fatal accident raises suspicions as a family fights to expose the real story.

programme.title: Accident, Suicide, or Murder

programme.desc: A teenager vanishes during a walk to the store on a cold winter night. The next morning police find her lifeless body in a snowbank six miles away.

programme.desc: David Dickinson and his team of dealers visit Liverpool Cathedral, where some girls give Henry Nicholls a run for his money, some photos land on Cheryl Hakeney's table and will Tony Geering let a 1930s cocktail shaker go to auction?

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David is joined by dealers Cheryl Hakeney, Henry Nicholls, Jan Keyne and Mark Stevens as they visit Congleton. Items of interest include a Lalique perfume bottle, a concertina and a gentleman's blotter.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David and his team are in St Albans where Simon Schneider learns about Mughal bling, Ian Towning has a date with `The Green Lady' and Karen Dalmeny gets to the bottom of an obscure Victorian stick.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David and his team attempt to sort the treasures from the trash in Skipton today. Jo Brayshaw gets a lesson on haggling from a lady keen to raise cash for her son's birthday.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David and the team are in Ilford for a `Text Santa' edition of the show. The dealers are Michael Hogben, Cheryl Hakeney and Jan Keyne as well as some special guests - a whole family of pearly kings and queens.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his team of antique dealers are in Gloucester where a beautiful heart-shaped pendant turns up on Debbie Serpell's table but can she afford to buy it? A letter seal that arrives on Stewart Hofgartner's table gets everyone's attention and some flying ducks land in the den.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson is in Ludlow with dealers David Hakeney, Helen Gardiner, Corrie Jeffery and David Ford, where items of interest include a watch with a connection to Margaret Thatcher and a striking Scottish vase, while an exciting gold deal tops one thousand pounds.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson travels to Matlock in Derbyshire with his team of Tim Hogarth, Brenda Haller, James Layte and Tony Geering. A set of Chinese paintings cause a stir in the room.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his dealers Corrie Jeffery, David Hakeney, Helen Gardiner and David Ford are in Ludlow in Shropshire, where items of interest include an Omega watch, a walking stick with an ivory handle carved into a dog's face, and a strange object called a potato flask.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his dealers Corrie Jeffery, David Hakeney, Helen Gardiner and David Ford are in Ludlow in Shropshire, where items of interest include an Omega watch, a walking stick with an ivory handle carved into a dog's face, and a strange object called a potato flask.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Airing from 2005 to 2009 on NBC, the game show series returns to primetime TV with original host Howie Mandel. A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

programme.desc: A Beatles fanatic challenges Dave to paint a modern Rolls-Royce to match the car John Lennon commissioned in the late 1960s.Will the modern version be able to match the psychedelic original.

programme.title: Kindig Customs
programme.desc: Dave and Kev take the show as far east as they have ever gone and head to Lake George, New York, for the Adirondack Nationals; the town shuts down for the car show, attracting some of the best on the east coast.

programme.title: Shed & Buried
programme.desc: Henry and Fuzz motor over to Taff's place in Lincoln. His extensive collection of Easy Rider-style chopper motorbikes includes an engraved 1970s Yamaha.

programme.title: Shed & Buried
programme.desc: Henry Cole and friends Fuzz Townshend and Guy Willison hit the road to find, buy and make a profit from the UK's abandoned automotive gems.

programme.title: Richard Hammond's Workshop
programme.desc: Richard and the team try to win one of their biggest ever restorations, a rare 1950s Porsche 365. Plus, the team head to Silverstone in search of racing glory.

programme.title: Richard Hammond's Workshop
programme.desc: Richard restores a unique 1947 Delahaye sports car belonging to legendary Grand Prix driver Louis Chiron. But with the Delahaye's original paint to track down, Richard soon fears his dream to display the car to the car elite may be in jeopardy.

programme.title: Richard Hammond's Workshop
programme.desc: Richard prepares the MGB for the racing season, and fixes up an E-type for a classic car rally. Kamla is delighted when Richard ups the workshop rate. But the plan is derailed when Neil takes on a rusting Lancia to raise money for endangered leopards.

programme.title: Incredible Engineering Blunders: Fixed

programme.title: Incredible Engineering Blunders: Fixed

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: Hunt for Extraterrestrials
programme.desc: Josh's journey continues on Easter Island, where some believe extraterrestrials visited a millennium ago. Then he visits the site of a recent meteorite strike in Zimbabwe.

programme.desc: In Hawaii the Raneys attempt to save two homesteaders whose dream homestead lays a flash-flood zone. As the weather closes in, can they save the property from the water?
